demographic_audit_note: Verified 79yo male patient in Atlantis; insurance confirmed as Straight Medicare. Corrected a typo in the home address (Street vs. Road) by auditing against the facility face sheet to ensure HHA and MA travel to the correct rural location.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker (SW) confirmed the goal is Home with intensive wound care and PT for amputation rehab.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: No, first time hearing of the company. Q2: Thinks we are providing the wheelchair. Q3: SW mentioned home health but patient was unclear on the start date. Q4: Expressed high anxiety regarding managing stump hygiene and phantom pain alone; PN escalated this to SW for additional counseling.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient successfully completed wheelchair transfer training. SW indicates discharge is targeted for Thursday. PN confirmed HHA referral was sent to 'Elite Home Care'.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into patient's son's smartphone; physical program flyer delivered to patient and placed in his discharge folder.
hha_confirmation_status: Initially 'Not In Place' due to Missing HHA Orders at Handoff; HHA intake reported they had the referral but no physician signatures for the wound care protocol. After PN escalation to SW, orders were refaxed. HHA is now 'In Place': SOC set for Friday 10:00 AM, Nurse assigned (RN Miller), 2-hour window confirmed.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (to SW): Confirmed D/C date is firm for Thursday afternoon and HHA has all signed orders. Call 2 (to Son): Confirmed son will be at the home for the Day 1 nurse arrival and understands to call the HHA coordinator if the nurse is not there by noon.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached son 24 hours prior to discharge; confirmed the hospital bed was delivered to the home and the son has the physical appointment card for the MA visit.
atlantis_final_sync: D/C date of Thursday and 1st MA visit date of Friday entered into Atlantis at 14:00.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for Friday at 1:00 PM (3 hours after HHA SOC start window). Booking was held until HHA confirmed RN assignment and SOC window.
narrative_summary: In Stage 1, the Connection Intake revealed significant patient anxiety regarding stump care, which was immediately escalated to the SW. During Stage 2 Maintenance, the PN identified a critical HHA readiness gap: the agency lacked signed wound care orders, preventing the 'In Place' status required for MA scheduling. By performing the Deep Dive HHA Barrier action, the PN categorized the delay and facilitated the SW's intervention. Once the HHA confirmed the nurse and time window, the PN completed the Confidence Audit and scheduled the MA, securing a successful service conversion.

format_1_state_log.event_description: HHA reports referral received but clinical orders for wound care are missing signatures.
PN identifies specific missing document (Stump Care Protocol) and flags to SW.
HHA confirms 'In Place' status after receiving signed orders; MA is scheduled.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient expresses fear of managing phantom pain and wound care during Connection Intake Q4.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated patient anxiety and caregiver concerns to the Social Worker for intervention.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA intake coordinator states they cannot set a SOC date because the wound care orders are incomplete.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted Deep Dive HHA Barrier check and notified SW of the specific missing signed protocol.

format_2_triples.situation: Discharge is 24 hours away and HHA has finally confirmed the nurse assignment.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted the pre-discharge pulse call to the son to confirm home readiness and MA visit time.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Upon hearing the HHA is missing orders, the PN logs the delay in Atlantis and waits for the weekly SW meeting to mention the status update.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW's role as the primary coordinator of orders but risks a missed discharge window if the SW is unaware of the HHA's specific intake hurdle.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN calls the HHA intake coordinator to identify exactly which orders are missing, then provides the SW with a specific request to refax the signed Stump Care Protocol to the agency.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Acts as a liaison by identifying the specific 'In Place' barrier and giving the SW an actionable task without overstepping into clinical management.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN contacts the attending surgeon's office directly, explains the urgency, and requests the surgeon's assistant to fax the signed home health orders directly to the HHA to ensure the MA can be scheduled today.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because the PN is directly managing clinical documentation and physician communication, which is the sole responsibility of the facility SW and clinical team.
